key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep network can use weld-pool images plus process parameters to predict penetration state, depth, and seam morphology during laser welding.
engineering_relevance: The method supports in-situ quality control by enabling early detection of weld defects and more reliable process monitoring in laser penetration welding.
method: Capture top weld-pool images with a CMOS camera during welding.
Fuse spatial-temporal features from images with welding parameters using CNNs and state-space models.
Train a multi-task model to jointly predict penetration state, penetration depth, and weld cross-section morphology.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on dataset construction and sensor consistency, so generalization to different materials, geometries, or welding setups should be validated.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
